meta.title: WD-EDU — Building Web Applications
meta.description: Remote learning platform for external students

header.searchPlaceholder: What do you want to learn?

home.headline: Remote learning platform for external students in "Web Application Development"
home.sub: Independent study in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript with practical labs, in-browser coding, and transparent progress tracking.
home.statsCourses: 40% less time spent searching for information
home.featureLearnDesc: Clear modules and assignments — Coursera-inspired navigation.
home.featureCodeDesc: Write, run, and check your work with simple automated validation.
home.featureProgressDesc: Scores, instructor comments, and a dedicated instructor console.
home.heroLeftTitle: Grow your skills. Shape your career.
home.heroLeftSub: Hands-on HTML, CSS, and JavaScript—with labs and an in-browser IDE in one place.
home.heroRightTitle: Train your cohort in skills that scale
home.heroRightSub: Course management, assignments, and results monitoring for instructors.
home.ctaRow1Desc: Course materials and lab instructions are centralized in one platform.
home.ctaRow2Desc: Structured modules reduce delays in practical assignments.
home.ctaRow3Desc: Autotesting and clear feedback improve completion quality.
home.promoBannerTitle: New on WD-EDU — all web modules in one hub
home.outcomeTitle: 90%+ of learners complete practice assignments successfully
home.outcomeSub: Autograding, clear briefs, and instructor comments improve outcomes.
home.testimonial1Text: “Clean UI and a browser editor that just works—perfect for studying remotely.”
home.testimonial2Text: “Coursera-like navigation and a dashboard that keeps me on track.”
home.testimonial3Text: “Three languages out of the box—huge plus for our cohort.”
home.faq1a: WD-EDU stores courses and grades. Official certificates are issued only according to university policy.
home.faq2a: HTML, CSS, JavaScript fundamentals, and practical web application tasks.
home.faq3a: Submissions run with timeouts on the server. For production, use a dedicated sandbox.
home.faq4a: The interface is responsive for phones and tablets.
